sanskrit: बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम्। धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ।।
transliteration: balaṃ balavatāṃ cāhaṃ kāma-rāga-vivarjitam | dharmāviruddho bhūteṣu kāmo ’smi bharatarṣabha ||
literal_meaning: I am the strength of the strong, devoid of passion and attachment; I am desire in beings that is not opposed to dharma, O best of Bharatas.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names two life-forces—strength and sexuality—and reframes them as sacred when stripped of selfish craving and aligned with ethical purpose.
psychological_state: Arjuna experiences a loosening of moral rigidity; the verse offers permission to own strength and sexual energy without guilt provided they serve a higher order.
trigger_scenario: A young athlete torn between performance-enhancing drugs and natural discipline, or a dating-app user conflicted about casual sex versus intentional relationship.
anchor_text: Use your strength and desire in service of what is right.
